Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants me to act as the target LLM receiving the provided prompt My task is to analyze the given declare model dictionary interpret the constraints and describe the underlying loan approval process in practical step by step terms I also need to explain how the constraints enforce compliance logic discuss real world motivations and speculate on the consequences of violating the rules

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Deconstruct the DECLARE Model Constraint Analysis Unconditional constraints existence Receive Application Gather Additional Documents must happen at least once absence Proceed Without Compliance must never happen exactly one Preliminary Credit Check must happen exactly once init Receive Application must be the very first step

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Binary Relational constraints response Receive Application to Preliminary Credit Check precedence Quality Assurance Review to Authorize Contract Terms succession Preliminary Credit Check to Gather Additional Documents coexistence Gather Additional Documents with Authorize Contract Terms

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait lets double check DECLARE semantics Actually responded existence usually means if A happens B must occur at least once in the trace Response means if A happens B must happen after A

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets re evaluate standard DECLARE definitions chainresponse If A occurs B must occur immediately after A chainprecedence B can only occur if A occurred immediately before B chainsuccession A occurs iff B occurs immediately after A

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is a contradiction in the provided model unless Authorize Contract Terms is the first step which violates init

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: noncoexistence means Transfer Funds and Receive Application cannot both exist in the same trace init requires Receive Application therefore Transfer Funds can never happen

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Hold on This model is full of intentional contradictions or I am misinterpreting the DECLARE syntax Lets review DECLARE syntax carefully

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets re read the model with this strict definition init Receive Application existence Receive Application and Gather Additional Documents absence Proceed Without Compliance exactly one Preliminary Credit Check response Receive Application to Preliminary Credit Check succession Preliminary Credit Check to Gather Additional Documents coexistence Gather Additional Documents with Authorize Contract Terms precedence Quality Assurance Review to Authorize Contract Terms altsuccession Gather Additional Documents to Quality Assurance Review responded existence Assemble Loan Offer Package to Quality Assurance Review chainsuccession Quality Assurance Review to Assemble Loan Offer Package chainresponse Assemble Loan Offer Package to Transfer Funds altprecedence Notify Customer to Transfer Funds altresponse Transfer Funds to Notify Customer chainprecedence Authorize Contract Terms to Preliminary Credit Check noncoexistence Transfer Funds to Receive Application nonsuccession Notify Customer to Preliminary Credit Check nonchainsuccession Authorize Contract Terms to Notify Customer

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is a hard logical contradiction in the provided model

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Its highly likely the prompt creator made a mistake in mapping the dictionary for example swapping source and target or misusing chainprecedence versus precedence or noncoexistence

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The prompt hint tells me the intended flow Receive Application Preliminary Credit Check Gather Additional Documents Quality Assurance Review Assemble Loan Offer Package Authorize Contract Terms Transfer Funds Notify Customer

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets map the constraints to this intended flow and see where the dictionary deviates then address it diplomatically

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is definitely a typo in the prompt model It probably meant noncoexistence with Proceed Without Compliance and Transfer Funds or some similar relation

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Strategy for the response Acknowledge and validate Start by outlining the intended practical loan approval workflow based on the activity descriptions and the prompt own hints Step by step practical description Walk through the ideal customer journey Constraint analysis Group the constraints by their business purpose Address model anomalies Gently point out that a strict literal execution of the provided DECLARE dictionary contains logical contradictions Explain why these are likely typos in the model generation and what the correct constraints should be

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Real world motivations Discuss regulatory compliance fraud prevention risk management and customer satisfaction Consequences of violation Speculate on what happens if rules are broken such as regulatory fines financial loss reputational damage

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Drafting the Content Section by Section Introduction Briefly state that the DECLARE model represents a declarative process specification for a loan application focusing on what must and must not happen rather than rigidly prescribing how

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Step by Step Practical Process The Ideal Flow Intake Customer submits application Receive Application Must be first Initial Vetting Bank runs a credit check Preliminary Credit Check Must follow application Exactly one check Documentation If the check passes more docs are gathered Gather Additional Documents Quality and Compliance The file undergoes a QA review Quality Assurance Review Offer Creation The loan package is assembled Assemble Loan Offer Package Final Approval Contract terms are authorized Authorize Contract Terms Fulfillment Funds are transferred Transfer Funds Communication Customer is notified Notify Customer

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Mapping Constraints to Business Logic and noting anomalies Mandatory Starting Points init existence Risk and Compliance absence exactly one Sequencing Rules response App to Credit Check succession Credit Check to Docs precedence QA to Authorize

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Anomaly 1 noncoexistence Transfer Funds Receive Application Literally this means a loan can never be funded if an application was received Correction This is likely a modeling error

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Anomaly 2 chainprecedence Authorize Contract Terms Preliminary Credit Check Literally this means a credit check can only happen immediately after contract authorization which is backwards and violates the init rule Correction Likely intended to be precedence Preliminary Credit Check Authorize Contract Terms

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Anomaly 3 altprecedence Notify Customer Transfer Funds versus altresponse Transfer Funds Notify Customer Together they form an alternating relation meaning Notify and Transfer must happen in that specific order While some banks notify of approval before funding the prompt hints at final disbursement and customer notification implying Transfer then Notify

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Real World Motivations Regulatory Compliance Avoiding Proceed Without Compliance prevents massive fines Fraud Prevention Gather Additional Documents and Quality Assurance Review ensure the applicant is who they say they are and the data is valid Risk Management Preliminary Credit Check prevents issuing bad debt exactly one prevents multiple hard pulls Customer Satisfaction Clear sequencing prevents the bank from asking for documents for an application that will be instantly denied or transferring funds without notifying the customer

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: Consequences of Violations Omitting QA leads to regulatory fines and approval of fraudulent loans Transferring funds before Authorization creates operational chaos and unrecoverable losses Skipping Credit Check increases default rates Proceeding Without Compliance leads to legal action loss of banking license and reputational destruction

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Review and Refine Tone Professional analytical consultative Clarity Ensure DECLARE terminology is explained simply for a business audience Completeness Did I answer all parts of the prompt Yes Self Correction during drafting Make sure I do not sound too critical of the prompt model Frame the anomalies as common modeling artifacts or likely typographical inversions
